description: Cycle A of prod-readiness plan (2026-05-25 audit) â€” closes 6 P0 HTTP-surface gaps before critical-prod cutover. Adds HTTP server timeouts, built-in TLS listener (single-cert + SNI multi-cert + hot-reload + k8s-mount aware), mTLS to all backend connections (Cassandra + TiKV + S3-upstream; RADOS via cephx doc-only), trusted-proxies-aware forwarded-header parsing, admin endpoint on separate listener with own TLS + optional mTLS, per-IP + per-key ingress rate limiter. ~18 new STRATA_* env vars wired through Config + envMap + strata.toml.example + /reference/env-vars.md per ralph/toml-parity contract. 10 stories. Pre-launch hard cutover OK â€” US-007 introduces README `## Breaking changes` section for forwarded-header default. Source PRD at tasks/prd-harden-gateway.md (deleted on cycle close per PRD lifecycle).

userStories.title: HTTP server timeouts + ROADMAP entry create
userStories.description: As an SRE, I want the gateway HTTP server to enforce per-connection timeouts so a slowloris attack or stuck client cannot exhaust the connection pool. As a maintainer, I want the ROADMAP entry for this cycle created up-front so the close-flip protocol from prior 12 cycles is preserved.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/serverapp/serverapp.go::Run` (current `&http.Server{Addr, Handler}` at line 281) sets 5 timeout fields: `ReadHeaderTimeout`, `ReadTimeout`, `WriteTimeout`, `IdleTimeout`, `MaxHeaderBytes`.

Defaults: `ReadHeaderTimeout=10s`, `ReadTimeout=60s`, `WriteTimeout=30m` (5GiB Ã· 30m â‰ˆ 2.8 MB/s minimum, cellular-safe; 5m was the original proposal, rejected as too aggressive), `IdleTimeout=120s`, `MaxHeaderBytes=1<<20`.

Env overrides: `STRATA_HTTP_READ_HEADER_TIMEOUT`, `STRATA_HTTP_READ_TIMEOUT`, `STRATA_HTTP_WRITE_TIMEOUT`, `STRATA_HTTP_IDLE_TIMEOUT`, `STRATA_HTTP_MAX_HEADER_BYTES`. Value `0` accepted = disabled (Go `http.Server` zero-value semantic; dev-mode use).

Wired through `Config.HTTP.*` substruct via koanf; `envMap` registry updated with 5 new entries (`STRATA_HTTP_READ_HEADER_TIMEOUT` â†’ `http.read_header_timeout`, etc); `internal/config/exempt_env_vars.go` NOT extended.
`deploy/strata.toml.example` gains `[http]` section with all 5 keys + one-line comment per knob.
`/reference/env-vars.md` TOML-key column populated for all 5 new rows.
Range validation: timeouts â‰¥ 0 (negative rejected; 0 = disabled). Upper bound `STRATA_HTTP_WRITE_TIMEOUT` 24h; `STRATA_HTTP_MAX_HEADER_BYTES` 16<<20 (16 MiB).

Unit test `internal/serverapp/timeouts_test.go`: (a) defaults set when envs unset, (b) custom envs override, (c) `STRATA_HTTP_WRITE_TIMEOUT=0` accepted = disabled, (d) negative env fails fast at boot.

**Slowloris chaos test** `internal/serverapp/timeouts_chaos_test.go` (Go-native, NOT shell-based `nc` â€” BusyBox semantics differ): opens raw TCP via `net.Dial`, writes one byte every 100ms past `ReadHeaderTimeout`, asserts connection dropped within `ReadHeaderTimeout + 5s` slack.

ROADMAP entry CREATED in `ROADMAP.md` under `## Correctness & consistency` section: `- **P0 â€” Cycle A: harden-gateway (HTTP timeouts + built-in TLS + backend mTLS + trusted proxies + admin listener split + ingress rate limit).** In progress on `ralph/harden-gateway`. Closes 6 P0 gaps from the 2026-05-25 prod-readiness audit. Flipped Done on cycle close (US-010).`
`internal/config/env_toml_parity_test.go` drift-lint stays green with new envs.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Built-in TLS listener â€” single-cert + min-version + cipher profile enum
userStories.description: As an operator, I want Strata to terminate TLS itself with operator-supplied certificates so I can deploy without an external TLS sidecar.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/serverapp/serverapp.go` branches between `ListenAndServe()` (default â€” empty cert envs) and `ListenAndServeTLS(cert, key)` (cert + key files set).
`STRATA_TLS_CERT_FILE` + `STRATA_TLS_KEY_FILE` paths read at boot; cert loaded via `tls.LoadX509KeyPair`.
`STRATA_TLS_MIN_VERSION` env: default `TLS1.2`, accepted values `TLS1.2` | `TLS1.3` only; rejection on other values at boot.

`STRATA_TLS_CIPHER_PROFILE` env (ENUM, NOT free-form cipher list â€” free-form would allow operators to accidentally select weak ciphers; Go's `tls` package only fail-fasts on unknown names, not weak ones). Accepted values: `mozilla-modern` (default â€” TLS 1.3 only ciphers: TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 / TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384 / TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256), `mozilla-intermediate` (TLS 1.2 + 1.3 AEAD ciphers per Mozilla SSL Config Generator Intermediate profile), `go-default` (current Go safe set via `tls.CipherSuites()`). Unknown profile fail-fast at boot.
When `STRATA_TLS_MIN_VERSION=TLS1.3`, `STRATA_TLS_CIPHER_PROFILE` is doc-noted as informational only (Go `tls` package does not honor cipher list for TLS 1.3 â€” fixed by RFC 8446).
Envs wired through `Config.TLS.*` substruct + `envMap` + TOML example `[tls]` section + reference page.

Unit test `internal/serverapp/tls_test.go`: (a) HTTPS handshake against in-memory test cert, (b) `STRATA_TLS_MIN_VERSION=TLS1.3` rejects TLS 1.2 client, (c) invalid cipher profile fails at boot, (d) `mozilla-modern` profile rejects RC4 client even when client offers it.
Smoke script `scripts/smoke-tls.sh` runs Strata with self-signed cert, validates HTTPS handshake + HTTP/2 negotiation + cert content via openssl s_client.
`make smoke` (TiKV-default lab, TLS not configured) still passes â€” opt-in default preserved.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: TLS SNI multi-cert + hot-reload (atomic.Pointer + k8s-mount aware)
userStories.description: As an operator running multi-tenant Strata, I want SNI-driven multi-certificate selection and certificate hot-reload so I can rotate certs via cert-manager without restarting the gateway.
acceptanceCriteria: `STRATA_TLS_CERT_DIR` env (mutually exclusive with single-cert envs from US-002 â€” fail-fast at boot if both set). Directory walked at boot for `*.crt` + matching `*.key` pairs; cert SAN/CN extracted; `tls.Config.GetCertificate` callback dispatches via SNI ServerName lookup.

**Cert store backed by `atomic.Pointer[map[string]*tls.Certificate]`** (NOT RWMutex â€” read-side is hot per-handshake; RWMutex contention measurable under load). Reload-side writes new pointer atomically; old pointer remains valid for in-flight handshakes (Go GC reclaims when no references).
**Hot-reload via fsnotify** on the cert dir AND single-cert paths from US-002. fsnotify v1.9.0 already in `go.sum` (transitive via koanf) â€” no new top-level require.

**k8s ConfigMap / Secret atomic-symlink-swap aware**: kubelet swaps target via symlink rename so fsnotify on the file itself misses the modify event. Watch shape: (a) single-cert: watch parent directory for RENAME + CREATE events on symlink basename; (b) `STRATA_TLS_CERT_DIR`: watch directory recursively for CREATE/RENAME/WRITE events for `*.crt` or `*.key`.

**Periodic reconciliation fallback** every 60s (fsnotify watchers can drop events under load): re-stat all cert files; if mtime / inode changed, reload. Knob `STRATA_TLS_RELOAD_INTERVAL` (default 60s, range [10s, 1h], 0 = disabled).
Optional client-cert verification: `STRATA_TLS_CLIENT_CA_FILE` (PEM) â€” when set, `ClientAuth = RequireAndVerifyClientCert` enabled.
Envs wired through `Config.TLS.*` substruct (extends US-002 substruct) + `envMap` + TOML example + reference page.

Unit test `internal/serverapp/tls_reload_test.go`: (a) SNI multi-cert dispatch picks correct cert per ServerName, (b) atomic swap on cert file modify continues in-flight handshakes with old cert + new handshakes get new cert, (c) k8s symlink-swap scenario (rename + symlink-target change) triggers reload, (d) periodic reconciliation catches missed fsnotify event (simulated by skipping fsnotify watcher entirely).

Integration test `internal/serverapp/tls_k8s_mount_test.go` (build tag `integration`): docker-driven test mounting a directory mimicking kubelet Secret-mount layout (`..data` symlink + atomic rotation); verifies hot-reload picks up cert change.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Cassandra mTLS (gocql SslOptions)
userStories.description: As a security-conscious operator, I want Strata to authenticate to Cassandra via mutual TLS so a network intruder cannot impersonate the gateway.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/meta/cassandra/store.go` `gocql.ClusterConfig` builder extended to wire `gocql.SslOptions` from env: `STRATA_CASSANDRA_TLS_CA_FILE`, `STRATA_CASSANDRA_TLS_CERT_FILE`, `STRATA_CASSANDRA_TLS_KEY_FILE`, `STRATA_CASSANDRA_TLS_SKIP_VERIFY` (default false; range bool).

`STRATA_CASSANDRA_TLS_SKIP_VERIFY=true` logs single WARN at boot (`Cassandra TLS verification disabled â€” never set in production`) + bumps gauge `strata_backend_tls_skip_verify{backend="cassandra"}=1`. New gauge added to `internal/metrics/`.
All four envs wired through `Config.Meta.Cassandra.TLS` substruct + `envMap` + TOML example `[meta.cassandra.tls]` section + reference page.

Integration test `internal/meta/cassandra/tls_integration_test.go` (build tag `integration`, testcontainers-based): provisions self-signed CA + Cassandra server cert + Strata client cert at test time; verifies connection succeeds with valid certs + fails with wrong CA + fails with no client cert when server requires.
Empty TLS envs â†’ no SslOptions = current plain-TCP behavior preserved.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: TiKV mTLS (config.Security)
userStories.description: As a security-conscious operator, I want Strata to authenticate to TiKV via mutual TLS so a network intruder cannot impersonate the gateway.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/meta/tikv/store.go` + `internal/meta/tikv/kv_tikv.go` config builder extended to populate `tikv-client-go` `config.Security` struct: `STRATA_TIKV_TLS_CA_FILE`, `STRATA_TIKV_TLS_CERT_FILE`, `STRATA_TIKV_TLS_KEY_FILE`, `STRATA_TIKV_TLS_SKIP_VERIFY`.
PD endpoint scheme respect: when TLS envs set, PD endpoint URLs accept `https://` scheme (`internal/meta/tikv/pdclient.go` http.Client extended with TLS config).
`STRATA_TIKV_TLS_SKIP_VERIFY=true` logs WARN + bumps gauge `strata_backend_tls_skip_verify{backend="tikv"}=1`.
All four envs wired through `Config.Meta.TiKV.TLS` substruct + `envMap` + TOML example `[meta.tikv.tls]` section + reference page.
Integration test `internal/meta/tikv/tls_integration_test.go` (build tag `integration`): provisions TLS-enabled PD + TiKV containers via testcontainers; verifies handshake.
Empty TLS envs â†’ no Security config = current plain-gRPC behavior preserved.

userStories.title: S3-upstream mTLS (global + per-cluster) + RADOS cephx doc note
userStories.description: As an operator using S3-pass-through, I want Strata to authenticate to the upstream S3 endpoint via mutual TLS â€” globally and per-cluster.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/data/s3/backend.go::connFor` extended: per-cluster `*awss3.Client` swaps `aws.Config.HTTPClient` with a `*http.Client` whose `Transport` carries a `*tls.Config` built from CA + client cert env.
**Global default** envs: `STRATA_S3_TLS_CA_FILE`, `STRATA_S3_TLS_CERT_FILE`, `STRATA_S3_TLS_KEY_FILE`, `STRATA_S3_TLS_SKIP_VERIFY` apply when no per-cluster TLS override is present.

**Per-cluster override** via existing `STRATA_S3_CLUSTERS` JSON schema (ralph/s3-multi-cluster contract from `internal/data/s3/Backend`): new optional `tls: {ca_file, cert_file, key_file, skip_verify}` field per cluster spec. **Resolution rule: per-cluster wins; global as fallback** â€” any single per-cluster TLS key replaces the global block entirely for that cluster (no merge to avoid surprise semantics).
Per-cluster `tls` field is additive â€” omitting it preserves backwards-compat; existing `STRATA_S3_CLUSTERS` JSON parses unchanged.
`STRATA_S3_TLS_SKIP_VERIFY=true` (either layer) logs WARN + bumps gauge `strata_backend_tls_skip_verify{backend="s3",cluster="<id>"}=1`.
All envs wired through `Config.Data.S3.TLS` substruct + `envMap` + TOML example `[data.s3.tls]` section + reference page.

**RADOS doc-only**: new section in `/operate/tls-termination.md` (created in US-010) titled `RADOS uses cephx, not TLS` explains cephx is the production-supported mutual-auth path; mTLS layer is N/A; no code change for RADOS. Placeholder doc-section stub added in this story for US-010 to fill in.
Unit test `internal/data/s3/tls_test.go` wraps `httptest.NewTLSServer` + verifies (a) global TLS config honored, (b) per-cluster override beats global, (c) skip_verify warn-and-bump fires.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Trusted proxies + replace blind-trust sites + README Breaking changes section
userStories.description: As a security-conscious operator, I want the gateway to ignore `X-Forwarded-*` headers from untrusted sources so a malicious client cannot spoof the `Secure` cookie flag or the source IP in audit logs.
acceptanceCriteria: New `internal/serverapp/trusted_proxies.go` package with `TrustedProxies` type carrying parsed `[]net.IPNet` slice + `Contains(remoteAddr string) bool` method.
Parsed at boot from `STRATA_TRUSTED_PROXIES` env (comma-separated CIDR list, e.g. `10.0.0.0/8,192.168.0.0/16`). Invalid CIDR â†’ fail-fast at boot with explicit error message naming the bad entry.
Default empty â†’ forwarded headers NEVER trusted. Audit log uses `r.RemoteAddr` directly; cookies require actual TLS termination on the Strata listener.

Replace ALL blind-trust call sites (audit-grep confirmed 2 today): (a) `internal/adminapi/auth.go:100` (`X-Forwarded-Proto` â†’ secure cookie flag), (b) `internal/s3api/notification.go:112` (`X-Forwarded-For` â†’ notification event source-ip field). Re-grep `grep -rEn 'X-Forwarded|X-Real-IP' internal/` at implementation time to catch any new sites added since 2026-05-25 audit.
When `remoteAddr` matches trusted CIDR: parse forwarded header per RFC 7239 left-to-right discipline (first untrusted hop is the original client).
When `remoteAddr` does NOT match: ignore the header entirely; fall back to `r.RemoteAddr`.
`Config.TrustedProxies` field wired through env + `envMap` + TOML example + reference page.

Unit test `internal/serverapp/trusted_proxies_test.go`: (a) empty trusted-list ignores forwarded headers, (b) trusted CIDR honors them, (c) untrusted source IP falls back to RemoteAddr, (d) malformed CIDR fails at boot, (e) IPv4 + IPv6 CIDR mix.

**README `## Breaking changes` section CREATED** â€” file currently has no such section. Insert between `## Quickstart` and the next section. Body: `X-Forwarded-Proto secure-cookie behavior changed in this release â€” operators behind a load balancer or ingress must set STRATA_TRUSTED_PROXIES to the proxy's source CIDR. Default empty = forwarded headers ignored (safe for direct exposure).` Per pre-launch policy, hard cutover acceptable.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Admin endpoint on separate listener
userStories.description: As an SRE, I want to bind the admin / console / metrics endpoints to a separate listener (loopback or RFC1918 only) so a public S3 client cannot reach the admin API.
acceptanceCriteria: New `STRATA_ADMIN_LISTEN` env (empty default = backwards-compat single-port shape preserved per pre-launch policy).
When set (e.g. `127.0.0.1:9001` or `:9001`), `internal/serverapp/serverapp.go::Run` builds a SECOND `http.Server` bound to `STRATA_ADMIN_LISTEN`.
Endpoints relocated to admin listener when split: `/admin/v1/*`, `/console/*`, `/metrics`, `/healthz`, `/readyz`. S3 surface (`/*` catch-all) stays on primary `cfg.Listen`.

Admin listener gets its OWN timeout block from US-001 family: `STRATA_ADMIN_HTTP_READ_HEADER_TIMEOUT`, `STRATA_ADMIN_HTTP_READ_TIMEOUT`, `STRATA_ADMIN_HTTP_WRITE_TIMEOUT`, `STRATA_ADMIN_HTTP_IDLE_TIMEOUT`, `STRATA_ADMIN_HTTP_MAX_HEADER_BYTES`. Defaults match US-001 except `WriteTimeout=2m` (no large multipart on admin).
Optional separate TLS for admin: `STRATA_ADMIN_TLS_CERT_FILE`, `STRATA_ADMIN_TLS_KEY_FILE`, `STRATA_ADMIN_TLS_CLIENT_CA_FILE`. When unset, admin runs plain HTTP (typical loopback shape).
When `STRATA_ADMIN_TLS_CLIENT_CA_FILE` set, admin listener requires mTLS for every connection.

Both listeners share the same OTel tracer + audit middleware + structured logger + access-log shape â€” middleware instances themselves shared (NOT re-wrapped per listener); `mux` per listener but middleware Handler chain identical.
Graceful shutdown drains BOTH listeners on SIGTERM within `cfg.ShutdownWait` via `sync.WaitGroup`.
All envs wired through `Config.AdminListen.*` substruct + `envMap` + TOML example (`[admin_listen]`, `[admin_tls]`, `[admin_http]`) + reference page.

Integration test `internal/serverapp/admin_listener_test.go`: (a) S3 request to admin listener â†’ 404, (b) `/admin/v1/clusters` to main listener â†’ 404, (c) both routes work when `STRATA_ADMIN_LISTEN` unset (backwards-compat), (d) admin listener bound to 127.0.0.1 rejects non-loopback connection, (e) SIGTERM drains both listeners within timeout.
Smoke script update: `scripts/smoke-tikv-default-lab.sh` keeps current single-port shape green; new `scripts/smoke-admin-split.sh` exercises the split shape.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Per-IP + per-key ingress rate limiter + go.mod direct requires
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to rate-limit requests per source IP and per access key so a runaway client cannot exhaust gateway resources.
acceptanceCriteria: New `internal/serverapp/rate_limit.go` package with `Limiter` type wrapping `golang.org/x/time/rate.Limiter` per (key|IP) entry, backed by fixed-size LRU via `github.com/hashicorp/golang-lru/v2` (Apache 2.0; ~300 LOC vetted dep).

**`go.mod` direct require added** for both `golang.org/x/time/rate` (currently transitive only via `internal/rebalance/throttle.go`) and `github.com/hashicorp/golang-lru/v2`. `go mod tidy` clean post-cycle.
Per-access-key limiter keyed on `auth.FromContext(ctx).AccessKey`; empty (anonymous mode) skips this limiter.
Per-IP limiter keyed on `r.RemoteAddr` (when no trusted proxy) OR resolved client IP from US-007 (when trusted-proxy CIDR matches).
`STRATA_RATE_LIMIT_PER_KEY` env (req/sec, default 0 = disabled, range [0, 100000]).
`STRATA_RATE_LIMIT_PER_IP` env (req/sec, default 0 = disabled, range [0, 100000]).
`STRATA_RATE_LIMIT_BURST` env (token-bucket burst capacity, default `2 Ã— limit`).
`STRATA_RATE_LIMIT_CACHE_SIZE` env (LRU cap, default 100000, range [1000, 10000000]).

Middleware order: rate limiter runs AFTER auth middleware (so per-key has resolved AccessKey) but BEFORE audit (so refused requests don't fill audit log). Wire site `serverapp.go:279` mux handler chain.
On refusal: HTTP 429 + `SlowDownRate limit exceeded` body (AWS S3 contract) + `Retry-After: 1` header + counter `strata_ingress_rate_limit_refused_total{reason="key"|"ip"}` increments.
LRU eviction on full: oldest entry evicted (forgets its token-bucket state â€” acceptable; conservatively evicted client gets a full bucket again).
Both limiters apply to S3 hot path only; admin/console/metrics/healthz/readyz endpoints (whether on main listener via single-port shape or on admin listener) bypass rate limiting.
All envs wired through `Config.RateLimit.*` substruct + `envMap` + TOML example `[rate_limit]` section + reference page.

Unit test `internal/serverapp/rate_limit_test.go`: (a) disabled (0) is no-op, (b) per-IP fires at threshold, (c) per-key fires at threshold, (d) burst absorbs short spikes, (e) LRU eviction works at cap.
Smoke script `scripts/smoke-rate-limit.sh` runs Strata with `STRATA_RATE_LIMIT_PER_IP=5` + `hey -c 10 -n 100`, verifies â‰¥80% of requests return 429.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Smoke + docs + ROADMAP flip Done
userStories.description: As an operator, I want end-to-end validation that all six hardening features work together + new operator-facing docs so I can confidently roll Strata to prod.
acceptanceCriteria: New `scripts/smoke-harden-gateway.sh` smoke script drives ALL six features together against the TiKV-default lab: boot Strata with HTTPS + admin-split + trusted-proxies + rate limit + mTLS to TiKV; probe HTTPS via `curl --cacert`; probe slowloris via Go test binary from US-001 (`go test -run TestSlowloris ./internal/serverapp/`) NOT via shell `nc`; probe rate-limit via `hey -c 10 -n 100` â†’ assert HTTP 429 + `SlowDown` + `Retry-After: 1`; probe admin listener bound to loopback only via `docker exec` from peer container â†’ assert connection refused on admin port from non-loopback; probe X-Forwarded-Proto trust â†’ spoof from untrusted IP (expected: ignored) + from trusted CIDR (expected: honored); probe backend mTLS via admin `/admin/v1/storage/meta` returns healthy meta backend.

New doc page `/operate/tls-termination.md`: 4 deploy shapes (A=plain HTTP behind ingress / B=Strata-terminated single-port / C=Strata-terminated split admin+S3 / D=Strata-terminated + backend mTLS) + cert provisioning recipes (cert-manager + Vault PKI + openssl) + per-backend mTLS section (Cassandra / TiKV / S3-upstream subsections from US-004/005/006) + RADOS cephx section (from US-006 placeholder filled in).
New doc page `/best-practices/production-hardening.md`: 12-line checklist linking each line to runbook section.
`/operate/tls-termination.md` recommends `STRATA_ADMIN_LISTEN=127.0.0.1:9001` for prod (defense-in-depth even with NetworkPolicy).
`make docs-build` green (no broken refs; mermaid renders for deploy-shape diagrams).
`/reference/env-vars.md` shows all new STRATA_* rows (~18 new entries from US-001..US-009) with TOML-key column populated.

`deploy/strata.toml.example` carries all new sections: `[http]`, `[tls]`, `[admin_listen]`, `[admin_tls]`, `[admin_http]`, `[rate_limit]`, `[trusted_proxies]`, `[meta.cassandra.tls]`, `[meta.tikv.tls]`, `[data.s3.tls]`.
Drift-lint test `internal/config/env_toml_parity_test.go` green (all new envs accounted for).

**ROADMAP entry FLIPPED to Done** with closing-SHA backfill (matches standard 12-cycle close protocol): `~~**P0 â€” Cycle A: harden-gateway (HTTP timeouts + built-in TLS + backend mTLS + trusted proxies + admin listener split + ingress rate limit).**~~ â€” **Done.** Shipped via the `ralph/harden-gateway` cycle (US-001..US-010). Closes 6 P0 gaps from the 2026-05-25 prod-readiness audit. ~18 new STRATA_* env vars wired through Config + TOML + reference page. Two new operator docs: [/operate/tls-termination](docs/site/content/operate/tls-termination.md) + [/best-practices/production-hardening](docs/site/content/best-practices/production-hardening.md). Smoke harness `make smoke-harden-gateway` exercises every feature end-to-end against the TiKV-default lab. README "Breaking changes" section created (X-Forwarded-Proto trust behavior change). (commit <SHA>)`
ALL existing smokes still pass: `make smoke`, `scripts/smoke-signed.sh`, `scripts/smoke-tikv-default-lab.sh`, every `scripts/smoke-*.sh` shipped.
All existing CI jobs still green (web-build, lint-build, unit, integration-cassandra, docker-build, e2e, e2e-full, e2e-ui, ci-tikv, ci-scylla).
Delete `tasks/prd-harden-gateway.md` in closing commit per [Pre-launch no deploys] memory + PRD lifecycle (markdown disposable; Ralph snapshot under `scripts/ralph/archive/` becomes canonical).
`make smoke-harden-gateway` Makefile target wired (chains lab bring-up + smoke script).
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
